question_title: What will be the value of "Euro Short-Term Rate: Volume-Weighted Trimmed Mean Rate" on 2026-03-26?
summary:
The €STR (Euro Short-Term Rate) on 2026-03-26 is forecast to remain very close to its current level of ~1.931%. The rate is tightly anchored to the ECB deposit rate (currently 2.00%) with a consistent spread of ~6.9 basis points below. The ECB meets on March 19 and is overwhelmingly expected to hold rates unchanged (>90% probability per ESTR forwards). The €STR has been extraordinarily stable (std = 0.0025) in the 1.921-1.936 range for 71+ consecutive trading days.

A critical timing detail: March 26 falls in the new reserve maintenance period (starting March 25), meaning any rate change on March 19 would already be effective. There is a small (~5%) tail risk of a surprise 25bp hike to 2.25% driven by Iran conflict/oil price surge, which would push €STR to ~2.18%. This creates a bimodal distribution where 95% of probability mass centers on 1.931% and 5% on ~2.18%. The P10-P90 range falls entirely within the hold scenario.

factors.description: ECB expected to hold rates at 2.00% on March 19 with >90% probability per ESTR forwards and analyst consensus. Multiple news sources confirm unchanged rates expected.
€STR has been extremely stable at ~1.931% for the entire Dec 2025-Mar 2026 period (71 obs, std = 0.0025), strongly anchored to the ECB deposit rate of 2.00%
March 26 falls in NEW maintenance period (starts March 25). If ECB hikes March 19, new rate takes effect March 25, directly affecting March 26 €STR value.
Small tail risk (~5%) of ECB surprise 25bp hike to 2.25% due to Middle East conflict and oil price surge. Would push €STR to ~2.18% on March 26.
No end-of-maintenance-period effects: March 26 is 2nd business day of new period (old period ends March 24), so no unusual liquidity distortions expected.

reasoning:
**Forecast: €STR on 2026-03-26**

The €STR is forecast to remain very close to **1.931%**, its stable level for the past 3+ months.

**Key findings:**
- The €STR is tightly anchored to the ECB deposit rate (2.00%), trading ~6.9bp below it
- The ECB meets March 19 and is overwhelmingly expected to hold rates (>90% probability)
- March 26 falls in a **new maintenance period** (starts March 25), so any rate change would already be effective
- ~5% tail risk of a surprise 25bp hike (Iran conflict/oil shock) that would push €STR to ~2.18%
- The distribution is bimodal but the 10th-90th percentiles all fall within the "hold" scenario given the low hike probability

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 1.926 |
| P20 | 1.928 |
| P40 | 1.930 |
| P60 | 1.933 |
| P80 | 1.935 |
| P90 | 1.938 |
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series ECBESTRVOLWGTTRMDMNRT once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=ECBESTRVOLWGTTRMDMNRT`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-26 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
